title: Comanthera tricostata
taxonomy: Comanthera tricostata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of monocotyledonous plants commonly known as pipeworts. Eriocaulaceae is a relatively small family, with about 2,000 species distributed across the world. Comanthera tricostata is a species native to Brazil, and it belongs to the genus Comanthera. The classification of Comanthera tricostata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Eriocaulaceae family.

intro: Comanthera tricostata, also known as the "Pride of Brazil," is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Comanthera tricostata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Comanthera tricostata is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support skin health and immune function.
